response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but the fit for this specific role is weak. Their professional C++ exposure is brief and dated (roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project), far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income domain experience, and their database background centers on MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle. The mandatory pillars of the role are largely unmet despite solid general engineering craftsmanship.
strengths: Deep overall engineering experience with strong testing/TDD and CI/CD practices
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang async services at Dunnhumby)
Broad polyglot skill set with some C/C++ and Java background
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old, not Windows-focused
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure
No SQL Server/Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth vs. mandatory 5–10 years
Strong general engineering fundamentals and craftsmanship
